sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, but they’ve scored in every game, including a strong 3-1 win over Chelsea. Leeds United also have inconsistent form with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their last 5, but their recent 3-1 victory against Wolverhampton highlights their attacking potential. Both teams struggle defensively, with neither managing a clean sheet in their recent outings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Brighton have the edge, remaining unbeaten with a 1-0 win at home in 2022 and a 2-2 draw away in 2023. Historically, matches between these sides tend to be tight, with under 2.5 goals in 7 of their last 8 meetings, suggesting a low-scoring affair could be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Brighton rank 8th in the league for goals scored (14) and excel in creating big chances (9th, 19 created), while their pressing game is notable (1st in possessions won in the final third). Leeds, despite a similar goal tally, struggle defensively (conceded 14 goals), and their away form has been inconsistent. Brighton’s superior league position (13th vs 15th) and home advantage could play a key role.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Brighton’s Danny Welbeck has been in fine form, netting 5 goals in his last 5 matches with an average Sofascore rating of 7.4, posing a significant threat to Leeds’ defense. Leeds will rely on their attacking structure, though no standout player has emerged recently in their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 49% chance for a Brighton win, combined with their historical dominance over Leeds and stronger home form, the Seagulls are slight favorites. However, Leeds’ ability to score on the road and Brighton’s defensive frailties (no clean sheets in 5) suggest both teams could find the net. Expect a competitive match with a narrow outcome.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Brighton & Hove Albion lead Leeds United 1-0 at the American Express Stadium in Falmer. The solitary goal came early in the match from Danny Welbeck, who finished with a right-footed strike in the 11th minute. Brighton have shown more attacking intent and clinical edge in the first half, capitalizing on their opportunities. Leeds, despite having a slight edge in possession, have struggled to create clear-cut chances and break through Brighton's defensive structure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Brighton's first-half dominance
halftimeSections.text: Brighton have been the more dangerous side in the first 45 minutes. They created a big chance and converted it through Welbeck, showcasing their efficiency in front of goal. Their frequent entries into the final third and penetrative moves into the penalty area have kept Leeds under constant pressure. Defensively, Brighton have been solid, winning a significant portion of aerial duels and maintaining composure at the back with key contributions from players like Lewis Dunk and Jan Paul van Hecke.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Leeds' struggles
halftimeSections.text: Leeds United, on the other hand, have found it difficult to impose themselves in this match so far. Despite holding a marginal possession advantage, they failed to register a single shot on target in the first half. Their inability to win duels, especially in the air, has hampered their efforts to build sustained attacks. While they managed some entries into the penalty area, they lacked the final product, with no big chances created to trouble Brighton's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half predominantly favored Brighton, especially in the opening stages and around the time of their goal, where they enjoyed consistent attacking spells. Leeds showed signs of life with a shift in momentum during the middle of the half, pushing forward with intent for a prolonged period. However, Brighton regained control later in the half with notable attacking phases, while Leeds struggled to maintain consistent pressure, only managing brief moments of threat towards the end of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Brighton, Danny Welbeck has been the standout performer with his goal and overall involvement in the attack. Mats Wieffer also played a crucial role with an assist and creative contributions in midfield. On the Leeds side, players like Brenden Aaronson and Sean Longstaff will need to step up in the second half to spark their attack. Their ability to create opportunities and support the frontline could determine whether Leeds can mount a comeback.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Brighton are likely to continue their proactive approach, seeking to exploit Leeds' defensive vulnerabilities with quick transitions and sustained pressure in the final third. Leeds, however, will need to be more aggressive and clinical if they hope to level the scoreline. A potential tactical adjustment or substitution could help them unlock Brighton's defense, but they must improve their finishing and duel-winning ability to turn the tide.
